_meta.flops_interpretation: Mask-based: dense FLOPs unchanged (graph unmodified). Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-backend support. Structured (#2): GFLOPs genuinely reduced — measured on rebuilt model. Comparison across types is NOT apples-to-apples for FLOPs; this is disclosed in the taxonomy.
_meta.structured_pruning_safety: Only internal Bottleneck width (conv1→conv2) is pruned. conv3, bn3, and downsample paths are untouched, guaranteeing residual-addition shape safety in all blocks.
_meta.movement_pruning_method: Importance = (1/N) Σ |∂L/∂w × w|  over N batches. Normalized to remove large-tensor bias. Ref: Molchanov et al. 2017.
_meta.lth_protocol: θ₀ saved at SEED=42 before training. θ_T = result of training θ₀ in this script (same trajectory). Mask from global magnitude pruning of θ_T. Ticket = m ⊙ θ₀ retrained with frozen mask. Ref: Frankle & Carlin 2019.

1_unstructured.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).

2_structured.flops_note: TRUE structured pruning: 30% of internal Bottleneck channels physically removed (conv1+bn1+conv2 per block). conv3, bn3, and downsample are untouched — residual shapes guaranteed valid. GFLOPs are genuinely reduced and measured by thop on the rebuilt model.

3_magnitude.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).

4_movement.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
4_movement.movement_note: Importance = |grad×weight| / n_batches (normalized Taylor 1st-order). Single full-epoch accumulation over the training set.

5_lottery_ticket.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
5_lottery_ticket.lth_note: Correct LTH protocol (Frankle & Carlin 2019). θ₀ saved at SEED=42 before any training. θ_T obtained by training θ₀ for 30 epochs in this run. Global magnitude mask at 50% sparsity applied to θ_T. Winning ticket = m ⊙ θ₀ retrained for 30 epochs with mask enforced after every gradient step.

6_iterative.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).

7_one_shot.flops_note: Mask-based pruning — computation graph is structurally unchanged. Dense FLOPs are identical to baseline. Effective FLOPs depend on sparse-kernel backend support (e.g. DeepSparse).
